body.u: When I was sixteen I left school and joined the merchant navy. After a year of training I started my first voyage on my first boat to Australia.
It took about two months to reach Australia. When we did I took my four weeks leave and visited my uncle and Aunt in Woonond new south Wales about 50 miles north of Sydney.

In four weeks I went back to my boat and we left for New Zealand. On my 25 birthday I got six weeks leave and went home to Sheerness where I was met at Sheerness docks by my mum and dad and girlfriend.

Six weeks later just before I left I was engaged to my girlfriend before I left I was engaged to my girlfriend before I left for New York on my boat. Seven months later my boat docked in at Liverpool Docks and I caught a train for London.
In about two hours I reached London and caught a train for Gravesend.

In half an hour I changed trains for Sheerness In 16 minutes I arrived at Sheerness train station. My family were not expecting me for another month. They were very pleased to see me. I went round to my girlfriend’s house and four days later we were married. We went on our honeymoon. I rented a flat in Sheerness three weeks later.
Another five weeks and I left for Newcastle to catch another boat to Canada.
Two weeks out of Newcastle I sent my wife some money in fact every two weeks I sent her £25 pound out of my £50 wages. I wrote to my wife and parents constantly and they wrote back.
In three months later we reached Torento I spent my three weeks leave on the boat mainly apart front a couple of days sightseeing and buying presents for my family and friends.
Four months later I docked at London and returned home for my 3 months leave. In three monthsmonths' time it was my 29 birthday. I left for Australia in about a year I came out of the Merchant Navy

The first thing I did was buy a house for my wife and our two kids. The next day I got a job up the steelworks in Sheerness taking home £65 a week and a part time job on the Sheerness lifeboat. In three yearsyears' time I got a “call out” for the life boat where we had to go out and rescue some people of a sinking yauhtyacht.
We managed to save everybody. In 2010. My two sons joined up in the merchant navy while my two daughters got secretarial jobs in Sittingbourne.

On my 50 birthday me and my wife went on holiday twoto Spain where we saw one of our sons in Madrid his boat had just got in. In two years home I left the steel works and became a security guard in the Sheerness docks. In 2022 my sons came out of the merchant navy, And every Saturday and Sunday I visited my grandchildren and sons and daughters.
At the age of 60 which was last week I retired. And bought a cottage in the country for me and my wife.
